app_purpose: AppFinisher Runner — a Node.js CLI utility that orchestrates an autonomous build-completion loop. It validates required AppFinisher framework files exist, builds a prompt for Claude Code, runs Claude Code via execSync, checks whether enforcement criteria (test pass rate + coverage) are met, and retries up to MAX_RETRIES (10) times until enforcement passes or retries are exhausted.
critical_paths: 1. Setup validation: validateSetup() checks /appfinisher/ folder exists and required files are present; fails fast with process.exit(1) if not.
2. Prompt generation: buildPrompt(retryCount) constructs the Claude Code prompt string with iteration context.
3. Claude execution: runClaude(prompt) invokes `claude code` via execSync with prompt on stdin; logs warning on error but does not abort loop.
4. Enforcement check: enforcementCheck() reads APPFINISHER_STATE.json + APPFINISHER_ENFORCEMENT.json; returns pass=false if pass_rate < 1 or coverage < threshold.
5. Run loop: runLoop() iterates 1..MAX_RETRIES, calling runClaude then enforcementCheck; exits on pass or after max retries.
known_limitations: main() called unconditionally at module bottom — must guard for testability
enforce.required_checks.coverage_minimum does not exist (BUG): coverage enforcement silently broken
runClaude() swallows execSync errors with only a warning — Claude failure is non-fatal
No structured logging/output beyond chalk console messages
external_dependencies: External: claude code CLI (must be installed and on PATH)
